But we just oftentimes, while we can do those things, almost add-ons, if you will, we don't treat those as, you know, parts of where we would typically start our engagements.

Our workflows are helping develop those questions, find rich answers to that, even scrape the citations.

And then reviewing the outputs, sometimes you need to refine based off of what they see in the outputs that may kick off a variety of different processes.
The point being is, every step along the way, it's not that you're just pushing buttons in workflows.

So, you know, the process for each account is going to be a little bit different based off of their requirements, but that's the rhythm where we're basically we're trying to get to a level of like at the very least like equivalent of daily publishing.

So you have that consistent flow within your account and like, you know, we'll find the different ways of looping you and what's happening to get your feedback or is it necessary in terms of other channels to consider.
Certainly we can experiment and help our customers in a variety of ways that tends to not be a core part of the initial offering.
But, you know, there are customers like for like a Udemy where we're helping them spin up a newsletter and that's a very like custom project and we're going super deep in it.
And there's just a lot that goes into creating.
That wouldn't be included into the conventional base level workstream.
It's just more of a value add of helping our customers in a variety of different ways.

But the thing is, going back to that statement of editorial content, why we like to start there, if we try to jump into those other things, we're just not oftentimes going to have the right foundation and context to make sure we're actually set up for success.
But largely, it's organic in focus.
We've done experimentation with social and email, et cetera.
We've even done sales enablement-based things for some of our accounts because our workflows can be leveraged in a variety of ways.
It's just that's not our core focus.
It is more how can we be a better partner to our customers and help them.
So it's really, core focus is editorial on our own site.
And then really, you guys are kind of weaving in sort of some of that competitive intelligence, keyword intelligence.
able able able to to to to be able to be
Understanding our stakeholders or our customers and what they want to learn about and so on.
So it's kind of like all those things coming together, you guys producing high quality content on a daily basis that we can then get on our site.
I think the metrics are, you know, they look compelling, at least on the, I think, whatever company was, Ramp or something, like showing all this organic traffic.
But that, I think, is you guys don't do traditional SEO in terms of link building.
You guys are a content house, right?
We do a bit of link building here and there.
It's not a huge focus of ours.
Yeah, do have to hop in there myself.
Good to meet you, Valerie.
You know, it's not a necessary core focus of ours, but we get provided as like a, hey, you know, for earlier stage domains that need some, you know, domain.
Right.
Yeah.
But what we found is, like, we have one client here, for example.
They had very low domain authority, very few backlinks, no blog before.
Working with us.
started working in January.
We did not do link building for them.
And we just, you know, took the approach similar to I ran through, just like, really, how do we create high quality content for you?
That's eventually accelerated.
Naturally, the backlinks they started to get, their traffic is now they're averaging 100 plus a day.
And we've been able to grow without doing that.
So like, I tend to not like try to push it, like we can do it if customers really want us to help.
it's a weird, backlinking is a weird rule.
To be honest, it's a mess that happened there.
so I was kind of a bubble 20 years ago.
I think it's very much legacy of how you think about it.
Yeah.
I think this is this is very compelling.
I would say that would be that was my big concern here is like, okay, well, we do have domain authority or at least someone because we've been around for a very long time.
We've published relatively consistently back in the day, like even as close as last year.
So I think we're just looking for someone that can help really facilitate to grow.
We have a complex stakeholder base.
have chief nursing officers, risk officers, IT, risk committees, nursing staff.
There's just a lot of stakeholders to get.
These are systems that affect, they're not just hospitals or care centers.
You've got corporate, you have facilities, facility directors, a lot of those people.
So for a small business, I think very clear, you know, you're in a small retail shop, you have a panic on, like you press the button and like, okay, alert 911 or whatever.
But for a large corporate environment, they have security, they have like, you know, cameras, tons of stuff.
So that sale is very, very complex.
The education piece, if we can get warming those leads online, it would be a very interesting business case.
How quickly, if I pitch this forward, you know, to the CEO, what's a realistic expectation, you know, here to see some impact?
See some ROI start to be generated, meaning either a marketing qualified lead comes through, whatever.
What could I use to make the business case forward?
Yeah, I'm happy to dig into that more with you.
I do have someone waiting for me internally on the other line.
But essentially, from the typical patterns we see is you'll see momentum from a variety of different metrics wise, usually around like month two of publishing or so.
So by month three is typically when you see that first like big uptick and compounding growth from there.
Usually by like month five or so, that's when you can start to understand and slice like where are you getting like you start to slice by like topics and like what's actually gaining traction, etc.
And that's where you can start to understand like where you're getting better page engagement.
How is that translating down funnel?
So it's usually about a six month process for where you're going to start to get to the phase of like, how is this translating to actual like signups?
That doesn't mean you won't get signed.

Before, it's just a matter of like stat sig essentially, like how do you have confidence that, you know, there is something here, but it tends to be, you know, important for us to try to blend the qual and the quant together to not just like sit back on our hands and wait for, I mean, it takes time.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory call between ROAR for Good and GrowthX to explore potential AI-driven content marketing partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [ROAR for Good is seeking to accelerate growth, particularly in the enterprise market, and needs external support for content creation and marketing.](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=312.0)
  - [GrowthX offers AI-powered content creation services with a focus on high-quality, persona-driven editorial content for organic growth.](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=488.0)
  - [GrowthX's process involves a detailed strategy sprint, persona development, and content planning before moving to rapid content creation.](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=1685.0)
  - [ROAR for Good is interested in pursuing this partnership, with a follow-up meeting scheduled to discuss specifics and potential budget.](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=2356.0)

## Topics

### ROAR for Good's Current Situation and Needs

  - [ROAR for Good provides panic button systems for healthcare and hospitality sectors](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=228.0)
  - [Recently expanding into enterprise market with more complex sales cycles](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=334.0)
  - [Current marketing efforts are limited, with Valerie as the sole marketing team member](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=362.0)
  - [Seeking external support to accelerate growth and generate more leads, particularly for enterprise clients](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=369.0)

### GrowthX's AI-Powered Content Creation Approach

  - [Combines AI workflows with human expertise for rapid, high-quality content creation](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=497.0)
  - [Focus on editorial content as the foundation, with potential for expansion to other channels](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)
  - [Detailed onboarding process including strategy sprint, persona development, and content planning](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=1692.0)
  - [Typical output ranges from 5-15 articles per week, with potential for higher volume in some cases](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=1621.0)
  - [Handles end-to-end process from strategy to content creation and publishing](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=1981.0)

### GrowthX's Team Structure and Workflow

  - [Led by experienced professionals, including former TechCrunch editor-in-chief](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=999.0)
  - [Utilizes AI workflows for research, content creation, and fact-checking](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=1092.0)
  - [Employs managing directors, managing editors, and writers to ensure quality and strategy alignment](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=1014.0)
  - [Collaborates with clients through Slack, Notion, and Airtable for efficient communication and feedback](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=1606.0)

### Expected Results and Timelines

  - [Initial momentum typically seen around month 2 of publishing](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=2308.0)
  - [Significant uptick in growth usually observed by month 3](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=2313.0)
  - [By month 5, able to analyze topic traction and page engagement](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=2325.0)
  - [Around 6 months to confidently assess impact on signups and leads](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=2331.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Schedule a follow-up call for next week to discuss ROAR for Good's specific business needs](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=2356.0)
  - [GrowthX to share the presentation deck with ROAR for Good](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=2412.0)
  - [Explore potential budget and prepare for ROAR for Good's July board meeting presentation](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=2368.0)
  - [GrowthX to provide more details on their platform capabilities, especially fact-checking processes](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=2392.0)
  - [ROAR for Good to consider the partnership for accelerating their content marketing efforts](https://fathom.video/share/A1fGpFgPocoLvyRVSjxUrtB67ma5gE1G?tab=summary&timestamp=2409.0)

action_items.description: Prepare questions/topics re: ROAR business specifics, budget considerations for next call w/ GrowthX. Gather info needed for July board meeting pitch
Schedule follow-up call w/ Sami for mid-next week. Prep to discuss ROAR business specifics, budget options, platform details, fact-checking process
Share presentation deck from today's meeting with Sami
